zh.lifeSummary: William Browne是Johannes aka John Browne和Annes Brome的兒子，出生在英國英格蘭艾可托培斯費爾德。他於1558年4月9日與Lewsie aka Louise Cooke在英國英格蘭艾可結婚。他們至少是15個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1599年2月27日在英國英格蘭艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登。

nameMeanings.name: Browne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Braune   or   Braun  .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Brown
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: generally a nickname referring to the color of the hair or complexion, Middle English br(o)un, from Old English brūn or Old French brun. This word is occasionally found in Old French, Middle English and Old Norse as a personal name or byname (Middle English personal name Brun, Broun, ancient Germanic Bruno, Old English Brūn, or possibly Old Norse Brúnn or Brúni). Brun- was also an ancient Germanic name-forming element. Some instances of Old English Brūn as a personal name may therefore be short forms of compound names such as Brūngar, Brūnwine, etc. As a Scottish and Irish name, it sometimes represents a translation of Gaelic Donn (see below). Brown (including in the senses below) is the fourth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below).
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Ó Duinn (see Dunn ) or for any of the many Irish and Scottish Gaelic names containing the element donn ‘brown-haired’ (also meaning ‘chieftain’), for example Donahue .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: William Browne was born in Toppesfield, Essex, England, United Kingdom as the son of Johannes aka John Browne and Annes Brome. He married Lewsie aka Louise Cooke on 9 April 1558, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 15 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 27 February 1599, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: William BrowneはイギリスイギリスエセックストップエスフィールドでJohannes aka John BrowneとAnnes Bromeの息子として生まれた。1558年4月9日にイギリスイギリスエセックスでLewsie aka Louise Cookeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも15人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1599年2月27日にイギリスイギリスエセックスサフロン ワルデンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Browne(은)는 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 토페스필드에서 Johannes aka John Browne(와)과 Annes Brome의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1558년 4월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 Lewsie aka Louise Cooke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1599년 2월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 사프런 월든에서 사망했다.
